prompt: Before activating a plugin in production, what should an administrator validate first?
choices: Whether the plugin can be selectively activated for one assignment group only
Plugin dependencies, potential data model impacts, and required roles
That all inactive users are deleted from the instance
Whether the plugin automatically disables update sets
explanation: Plugin activation can introduce dependent functionality and schema changes, so admins should review dependencies, impacts, and role requirements before enabling it.

prompt: Which statement best describes plugin deactivation behavior in ServiceNow?
choices: Any plugin can be safely deactivated after one business day
Deactivation is automatic when no records use plugin tables
Many plugins are intended as one-way activations and are not routinely deactivated
Deactivating a plugin only hides menus and never affects configuration
explanation: ServiceNow treats many plugins as one-way activations because enabling them can add artifacts and dependencies that are not designed for routine rollback.

prompt: What is the recommended naming convention for a custom system property created by an administrator?
choices: Use a unique, app-specific prefix such as x_company_app.setting_name
Reuse a global glide.* property name to keep naming simple
Name it after the admin user who created it
Use uppercase only and include spaces for readability
explanation: Custom properties should use a unique namespace-style prefix so they are distinguishable from baseline properties and easier to govern across environments.

prompt: When storing an integration credential in a system property, which property type is most appropriate?
choices: String
Integer
True/False
Password (2 Way Encrypted)
explanation: Sensitive secrets should be stored in an encrypted password property type rather than plain text values.

prompt: How does instance time zone configuration affect date/time values in ServiceNow?
choices: It converts stored values from UTC to local time permanently in the database
It affects how date/time values are displayed, while values are stored in UTC
It changes only SLA schedules and nothing else
It applies only to reports, not forms or lists
explanation: ServiceNow stores date/time in UTC and applies time zone settings for user-facing display and interpretation.

prompt: Why is application scope important when building a custom app?
choices: It forces all scripts to run only on client side
It removes the need for role-based access control
It namespaces app artifacts to reduce collisions and control cross-scope access
It automatically publishes the app to the Store
explanation: Scope provides namespace isolation for app artifacts and supports governance of how resources are shared across applications.

prompt: In Studio, what does the Application Files list primarily represent?
choices: The configuration records that belong to the current application
Only update set metadata from all applications
All records in the global scope tables
A list of active user sessions in the instance
explanation: Application Files shows artifacts owned by the current app, helping developers manage and package app-specific records.

prompt: Which table setting allows developers in other application scopes to create business rules or client scripts on that table?
choices: Can read
Accessible from = This application scope only
Allow configuration
Allow access to this table via web services
explanation: Allow configuration governs design-time artifact creation on the table from outside the owning scope.

prompt: A script include must be callable from another scope. Which setting should a developer configure?
choices: Set the script include to client callable only
Disable protection policy
Mark the script include as Active = false
Set Accessible from to All application scopes
explanation: Cross-scope use of a script include requires its accessibility to include other application scopes.

prompt: What is the purpose of design-time application access controls on app data tables?
choices: To define whether other scopes can create and modify configuration artifacts on those tables
To schedule nightly table re-index operations
To encrypt all columns by default
To prevent all runtime GlideRecord operations
explanation: Design-time controls manage how other scopes can configure artifacts against a table, complementing runtime data access controls.

prompt: What is the main purpose of declaring an incident as a major incident?
choices: To bypass incident state transitions permanently
To trigger prioritized coordination and communication for high business impact disruptions
To convert all related incidents into service requests
To disable SLA tracking while the incident is open
explanation: Major Incident Management formalizes urgent, high-impact response with coordinated communication and accelerated handling.

prompt: How are incident tasks typically used during major incident response?
choices: They break response work into assignable, trackable activities under the parent incident
They replace the need for assignment groups
They are created only after the incident is resolved
They are limited to knowledge article approvals
explanation: Incident tasks allow teams to coordinate parallel technical actions while maintaining traceability to the parent incident.

prompt: Which configuration determines when an incident SLA pauses or resumes?
choices: The incident category dictionary attributes
The update set state
The pause conditions defined on the SLA definition
The major incident proposal workflow
explanation: SLA definitions include explicit start, stop, and pause conditions that control SLA timing behavior on tasks.

prompt: Which statement about SLAs and incident tasks is accurate?
choices: SLAs can target only the Incident table, not child tasks
Incident task SLAs are controlled only by assignment rules
SLA timers always stop when a related problem record is created
SLAs can be defined for incident tasks when conditions match that task type
explanation: Task SLAs are condition-based and can be applied to incident tasks as well as incidents when definitions and criteria align.

prompt: During a major incident, why is maintaining timely work notes and updates critical?
choices: It supports coordinated response, stakeholder communication, and auditability of actions
It allows agents to skip incident closure requirements
It automatically shortens all active SLAs
It prevents creation of child incident tasks
explanation: Consistent updates provide a shared operational timeline for responders, stakeholders, and post-incident review.
